hero.mission: A free, open learning community built by developers, for developers. No gatekeeping, no pointless grind — just real progress and the joy of building. Knowledge is a ladder to freedom, not a cage.

join.title: Join the Resistance
join.body: Connect with thousands of developers who are reclaiming their passion for technology.

ai.title: AI
ai.desc: Math foundations, LLMs, training & inference, benchmarks, datasets

cs.title: Computer Science
cs.desc: Data structures, algorithms, and core CS fundamentals

jobs.title: Interview Prep
jobs.desc: Give me a job. I'll do anything.

community.title: Community Notes
community.desc: Written by the people, for the people

footer.brand: A free, open learning community built by developers. We believe collective effort and radical openness can break down knowledge monopolies — and build something fairer.

notFound.heading: Page Not Found
notFound.body: The page you are looking for might have been removed, had its name changed, or is temporarily unavailable.

login.heading: Sign In
login.subheading: Sign in to access protected pages

toast.tokenMissingSave: Session expired — sign in again before saving
toast.loadError: Failed to load preferences — showing defaults

welcome.prompt: What can I help you with today?

summarize.title: Summarize this page

summarize.action: Summarize the main content and key points of this page

learning.action: Based on this content, what learning path would you suggest for a beginner?

apiKey.save: Save API Key to local storage

warning.title: Security Notice
warning.plaintext: The API key will be stored in plain text in your browser's localStorage
warning.access: Anyone with access to this browser may retrieve your API key
warning.shared: Do not check this on shared or public computers
warning.rotate: Rotate your API key regularly for better security

intern.notice: InternLM is provided by Shanghai AI Lab. No API key required — it's pre-configured and free to use.

dialog.title: Submit an Article
dialog.description: Select a section (single-select, searchable; top-level is for navigation only), or create a new sub-section, then jump to GitHub to create your article.

filenameError.invalid: Filename must start with a letter or number and contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, or underscores.

feed.title: Community Feed
feed.subtitle: Good reads curated by the community — paste a link, share the knowledge.
feed.empty: No approved shares yet. Be the first to submit one!

submit.title: Drop a Link
submit.successToast: Submitted! AI is reviewing your link...
submit.loginRequired: Sign in to submit a link

empty.title: No projects or reading list yet.
empty.subtitle: GitHub repos and doc contributions still shown below.

repos.heading: GitHub Repositories
repos.subtitle: Public repos · sorted by stars & updates

auth.required: Sign in to edit your profile.
auth.notYours: This isn't your profile (URL ID: {id}). Only the owner can edit.

intro.label: About this page
intro.body: What you put here shows up on your profile page at /u/{id}. Every field is optional — fill what you like; empty profiles still show GitHub info and contribution stats.

error.noToken: Login token missing, please sign in again

sec1.title: SEC. 01 · IDENTITY
sec1.heading: About You
sec1.description: A one-line tagline and a short bio. Shown at the top of your profile left column — think of it as your site-wide business card.

tagline.label: Tagline (≤80 chars)
tagline.placeholder: e.g. LLM Safety @ UNSW · looking for summer internships

bio.label: Bio (80-200 chars recommended)
bio.placeholder: Who you are, what you're working on, what you're interested in. e.g. CS MSc, working on alignment, writing tools on the side; happy to chat about RLHF / evals / infra.

sec2.title: SEC. 02 · LINKS
sec2.heading: Links
sec2.description: External places people can reach you or learn more. Up to 5 items; shown as small buttons at the bottom of your profile left column.

label.placeholder: Button label, e.g. Blog / Twitter / Zhihu

sec3.title: SEC. 03 · PROJECTS
sec3.heading: Your Projects
sec3.description: Personal projects / toys / open-source work. Your public GitHub repos are shown automatically below; use this for things not on GitHub or worth highlighting. Up to 8 items.

desc.placeholder: 1-2 sentences on what it does. Expands on hover.

tags.placeholder: Comma-separated tech tags, e.g. TypeScript, Next.js, LLM

sec4.title: SEC. 04 · PAPERS
sec4.heading: Currently Reading / Recommended Papers
sec4.description: Papers you've recently read and want to share (academic or tech-blog). Add a Zotero itemKey to auto-fill metadata, or type title / authors / year manually. Optional.

itemKey.placeholder: Zotero itemKey (optional — auto-fills metadata; or leave empty and fill below)

userMenu.switchGithub: Switch GitHub account (opens GitHub logout in new tab)
